info.title: dosbox-automation control API
info.description:
REST API for programmatic control of dosbox-automation. Automate installations, drive games, capture frames, and record or replay input.

The API is intended for local automation. By default the server binds to 127.0.0.1 and is not designed to be exposed to untrusted networks. Every endpoint under /api/v1 requires a bearer token; the documentation assets (this page, the landing page, openapi.json) are served without a token so the docs are browsable. Use the Authorize button to paste your token before running a request.

All endpoints read or write internal state atomically between emulated CPU steps. They never run during native DOS functions or interrupts, so the internal data structures are always consistent.

tags.name: Recording
tags.description: Capture and replay of input sequences.

tags.name: Drive
tags.description: Disk image mounting and disk swaps.

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Memory read, write, allocate, and free.

get.description: Returns the version string and a map of feature flags indicating which API groups are available. MCP clients use this to discover capabilities before calling endpoints.

features.description: Feature flags for API capability discovery.

get.summary: DOS data structures and MCB memory map
get.description: Returns pointers to internal DOS structures and walks the MCB chain to build a memory map of all allocated blocks.

200.description: List of lists, DOS swappable area, first shell, and MCB chain.

memoryMap.description: MCB chain walk. Each entry is one memory control block.

get.description: Reads `len` bytes from the given address. Parameters accept hex strings when prefixed with `0x`. The default response is raw binary; send `Accept: application/json` to get the data Base64-encoded in JSON.
Same as the unsegmented read. The segment may be a segment register name or a number.

put.description: Writes to the given address. Accepts raw bytes with `Content-Type: application/octet-stream` or a JSON object with a Base64 `data` field. The `If-Match` header (Base64) performs an atomic compare-and-swap and returns 412 when the current bytes do not match.

post.summary: Inject a sequence of input events
post.description: Events with `t > 0` are scheduled via the PIC timer for cycle-accurate delivery. Returns 400 for unknown key names, event types, or button names.

post.summary: Toggle pause on the active recording
Stop recording and return captured events
post.description: The returned events can be replayed via /api/v1/input/sequence.

get.description:
Returns the current rendered frame. The format is chosen by the `format` query parameter or the `Accept` header. This is the clean emulator output and does not include the on-screen automation overlay (OSD). Returns 503 if no frame has been rendered yet.

The raw format is a binary header (uint32 width, uint32 height, int32 pitch, uint8 pixel_format, uint16 palette_count) followed by palette data when paletted, then the image data.

Reads the text-mode character buffer and returns it as UTF-8, with CP437 box-drawing and shade glyphs preserved. Empty text with is_text_mode false in a graphics mode. Use this to drive installers by screen state instead of fixed timings.

post.summary: Mount or swap a disk image on a drive
post.description: Mount or swap a floppy or hard disk image on a drive letter. For multi-disk installs, call this when the installer prompts for the next disk.

403.description: Mount blocked by policy (mounts locked or path not allowed).

post.description: The request body is the Lua source. Loading replaces any previously loaded script.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: A name for the script, used in status and logs.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description: Optional PRNG seed for deterministic runs.

parameters.name: debug
parameters.description: Enable trace logging for this script.

400.description: No script loaded, or already running.

200.description: Script state. `output` mirrors the script's dosbox.output table when present.

mode.description: What feeds the encoder: 'raw' captures the emulator framebuffer at native resolution, 'rendered' captures the post-shader output at window resolution as shown on screen.

last_stop_reason.description: Why the most recent video capture ended. 'none' means no capture has ended yet; 'write_error' covers failed writes and a failed final flush; 'disk_space_low' means the recording was stopped cleanly before the capture drive ran out of space (capture_min_free_space_mb setting).

mode.description: Capture mode of the running recording, or of the most recently started one.

post.summary: Type a string with paced key injection
post.description: Translates each character to its key equivalent and injects them paced to avoid overflowing the keyboard buffer. The optional cps parameter controls characters per second. Maximum 4096 characters per call.

409.description: Injection failed (e.g. another type in progress).

post.description: Scans a memory range for all occurrences of a value. The width selects byte (1), word (2), or dword (4) matching. Maximum scan span is 16 MB.

matches.description: Physical addresses where the value was found.

post.description: Pins a memory location to a fixed value. The value is rewritten every emulation frame. Calling again with the same address updates the frozen value.

delete.description: With no body or without an address field, clears all freezes. With an address field, removes only that one.

address.description: Remove only this freeze. Omit to clear all.

get.description: Reads a byte or word from an I/O port. The port number accepts hex with a 0x prefix.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port number (0x0000..0xFFFF). Hex with 0x prefix accepted.

put.description: Writes a byte or word to an I/O port.
Sets a general-purpose or segment register. General registers: eax, ebx, ecx, edx, esi, edi, esp, ebp. Segment registers: cs, ds, es, ss, fs, gs (values must be 0..0xFFFF).

bearerAuth.description: The API token printed to the log at startup (or written to the token file). Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

MemorySegment.name: segment
MemorySegment.description: Segment register name or a number. Hex allowed with a 0x prefix.

MemoryOffset.name: offset
MemoryOffset.description: Address offset. Hex allowed with a 0x prefix.

MemoryLen.name: len
MemoryLen.description: Number of bytes to read. Hex allowed with a 0x prefix.

IfMatch.name: If-Match
IfMatch.description: Base64-encoded expected current bytes for an atomic compare-and-swap.

Registers.description: 32-bit general registers, EIP, flags, and 16-bit segment registers.

strategy.description: The XMS allocator only supports BEST_FIT.

InputEvent.description: One input event. Unknown fields are rejected with a 400 naming the allowed fields for the event type.

t.description: Absolute position on the sequence timeline in milliseconds (recording format). Mutually exclusive with 'delay_ms'.

key.description: Key name, e.g. KBD_enter. Used by key events.

pressed.description: Press (true) or release (false). Used by key and mouse_button events.

x_rel.description: Horizontal mouse delta in pixels, positive right (mouse_move events). Mouse movement is relative only; there is no absolute positioning.

y_rel.description: Relative Y motion. Used by mouse_move.

delay_ms.description: Wait this many milliseconds after the previous event before firing (relative timing; the natural form for hand-written sequences). Mutually exclusive with 't'.

frame.description: Frame number for frame-locked replay (recording format).

x_abs.description: Recording metadata; not used for injection.

y_abs.description: Recording metadata; not used for injection.

is_text_mode.description: False in graphics modes; text is empty then.

columns.description: Live column count from the BIOS data area.

text.description: Screen contents, rows separated by newlines, CP437 converted to UTF-8.

error.description: Present only when the script errored.

output.description: The script's dosbox.output table, when set.

start.description: Physical start address of the scan range.

end.description: Physical end address (exclusive). Max span 16 MB.

McbBlock.description: One DOS Memory Control Block from the MCB chain.

type.description: M for middle, Z for last block.

pspSegment.description: PSP segment of the owning process.

filename.description: Owner filename from the MCB header.

isLast.description: True if this is the last block in the chain.
